  • When: March to June (young leaves). Autumn produces a second growth that is also usable.
  • Where: Everywhere disturbed ground meets moisture — path edges, stream banks, garden borders, the edges of agricultural land. One of the most widely distributed plants in the region.
  • Identification: Distinctive toothed leaves with fine stinging hairs visible on both surfaces. The sting is immediate and unmistakeable — wear gloves or harvest by grasping from below where the sting hairs are less dense. Cooking, drying, or blending neutralises the sting entirely.
  • No dangerous lookalike. Nothing else stings like nettles in this region.
  • Use: Blanch briefly (30 seconds) to neutralise sting. Use as a spinach substitute in everything: soup (kopriva čorba — a traditional Bosnian preparation), pasta filling, sautéed with garlic and olive oil, blended into pesto. Nutritionally superior to cultivated spinach — higher iron and vitamin C content.

Porcini / Cep (Vrganj — Boletus edulis)

  • When: September to November, peaking in October after autumn rain.
  • Where: Mixed forests with spruce, pine, and beech. The forests of Bjelašnica and Jahorina are the primary Sarajevo-accessible locations. Trebević also produces them.
  • Identification: Brown cap (chestnut to dark brown), white to cream pores underneath (not gills — tubes that look like a fine sponge). White to pale cream stem with fine net-like pattern. Cut cross-section: white flesh that does not change colour on exposure to air. The smell is distinctive — earthy, nutty, strongly mushroomy.
  • The dangerous lookalikes: Satan's bolete (Boletus satanas) — pores turn red/orange rather than white/cream. Bitter bolete (Tylopilus felleus) — flesh is white but tastes intensely bitter. Always taste a tiny amount of raw flesh if uncertain — Boletus edulis tastes mild and pleasant; bitter bolete is immediately unpleasantly bitter.
  • Use: Sautéed in butter with garlic and thyme. Dried for intense concentrated flavour in sauces and risotto. Preserved in olive oil. The finest edible mushroom available in the region and one of the finest in the world.

Chanterelle (Lisičarka — Cantharellus cibarius)

  • When: July to October.
  • Where: Mixed woodland, particularly under beech and oak. Bjelašnica and Trebević both produce them reliably after rain.
  • Identification: Vivid egg-yellow to orange colour. False gills (forked ridges running down the stem — not separate blade-like gills). Fruity, apricot-like smell. Firm flesh that does not crumble. The colour and false-gill structure together make identification reliable.
  • The dangerous lookalike: False chanterelle (Hygrophoropsis aurantiaca) — has true gills (separate blades), brighter orange colour, and no apricot smell. Jack-o'-lantern mushroom (Omphalotus olearius) — grows in clusters from wood, not singly from soil. Know these two and chanterelles are safe to identify.
  • Use: Butter, cream, eggs. The classic chanterelle preparation: sautéed in butter with shallots, finished with cream and thyme. Simple enough to showcase the flavour; complex enough to be genuinely special.

The Forager's Equipment

  • A wicker basket: Not a plastic bag. Spores fall through the basket weave as you walk, seeding new growth. Plastic bags cause mushrooms to sweat and deteriorate rapidly.
  • A small knife: For cutting mushrooms at the base rather than pulling (preserves the mycelium) and for cleaning in the field.
  • A field guide specific to the region: "Gljive Bosne i Hercegovine" (Mushrooms of Bosnia and Herzegovina) by domestic authors is the correct reference — species present in this region, not a pan-European guide that includes species not found here. Available at Buybook in BBI Centre.
  • A mentor for the first season: Foraging with an experienced local for the first autumn mushroom season is the most valuable single investment. The Sarajevo hills carry an active foraging culture — someone among your acquaintances forages. Ask to join them once. The lived knowledge transfers in ways that field guides cannot.

Preservation Methods

  • Drying: The primary preservation method for mushrooms and herbs. Thread mushrooms on string or lay on a rack in a warm, well-ventilated space (or in a low oven at 50°C with the door ajar). Dried porcini concentrated to a quarter of their fresh weight produce an intensely flavoured ingredient that reconstitutes in water.
  • Wild garlic pesto: Blended with olive oil, pine nuts (or walnuts), Parmesan, salt. Poured into sterilised jars, covered with a layer of oil, refrigerated — keeps 2–3 weeks. Frozen in ice cube trays — keeps 6 months.
  • Elderflower cordial: Recipe in the Beverages tab. The flowers must be used within 24 hours of picking — they ferment rapidly. Make the cordial the day of harvest.
The man who knows what grows in his own hills inhabits them differently from the one who does not. The Trebević forest in October, to a person who can identify what grows there, is not a park. It is a pantry. This knowledge was ordinary in Sarajevo a generation ago. It is worth recovering.— Von Lycaon

The Cultural Context

Foraging in Bosnia is not a hobby or a trend — it is a cultural practice with deep roots in both necessity and tradition. The pečurkarenje (mushroom picking) culture in particular is a significant autumn social ritual: families, entire extended groups, leaving the city for the mountains on weekends in September and October with baskets, meeting in the forest, competing gently for the best spots, returning with food for the week. This culture was maintained through the Ottoman period, through Yugoslavia, and through the post-war recovery. A Sarajevo resident who does not participate in it is missing one of the city's most coherent cultural expressions.

  • Knowledge transmission: Foraging knowledge in Bosnia is transmitted primarily within families — grandmothers to grandchildren, fathers to sons. The knowledge is experiential and local rather than textual and general. This means that a field guide alone is insufficient for the mushroom season — accompanying someone with local knowledge of specific sites and specific species is the correct way to begin. The field guide then becomes a reference tool for confirming what you already believe you have identified, not a primary identification tool used alone in the field.
  • Respect for the commons: The forests around Sarajevo are shared resources. The practice of cutting mushroom stems rather than pulling (preserving the mycelium), not taking immature specimens (allowing them to mature and spore), and not revealing productive spots publicly (so that they are not stripped in a single season) are the informal rules of the foraging commons. They are the difference between a sustainable harvest and a depleted one.
The ability to spend a night in the mountains with comfort and competence is not a survival skill — it is a quality-of-life skill. Sarajevo sits within an hour of some of the finest mountain terrain in the Balkans. Bjelašnica, Jahorina, Trebević, Treskavica, and the Sutjeska National Park gorge are accessible to a Sarajevo resident in ways that would require a flight from most European cities. This tab is the practical guide to using them properly.— Von Lycaon

The Wilderness Mindset

Competence in the outdoors follows the same principle as competence in everything else in this Codex: preparation and knowledge reduce risk to acceptable levels, while ignorance converts manageable situations into dangerous ones. The Bosnian mountains are not technically demanding by alpine standards — but they are genuinely remote, the weather changes rapidly, and mobile signal is absent in many areas. The man who is prepared for these conditions has a profound experience. The man who is not has a dangerous one.

The Essential Kit

Shelter

A three-season tent rated to at least -5°C for Bosnian mountain use. Recommended: MSR Hubba Hubba or Naturehike Cloud-Up series (available on eMag.rs, 150-400 BAM). The tent must be freestanding and able to be pitched quickly in wind. The inner should be mesh with a full rainfly — Bosnian mountain weather can deliver rain at any time of year. Practice pitching it in your garden before the first overnight.

Sleeping System

Sleeping bag rated to at least -5°C for summer Bosnian mountain use, -10°C for spring and autumn. Down is warmer per gram but loses insulation when wet. Synthetic is heavier but maintains warmth when damp. For the Bosnian context — high humidity, frequent morning dew — a synthetic bag or a down bag in a waterproof compression sack is the correct choice. A sleeping mat (foam or inflatable) is not optional — ground contact removes more heat than any cold air exposure.

Navigation

A paper topographic map of the specific area (available at mountain club huts and some bookshops) and the ability to use it. The Maps.me app with downloaded offline maps of BiH provides GPS navigation in the absence of signal. A compass to verify map orientation when lost. The three together — map, compass, GPS — provide triple redundancy. A single navigation method that fails leaves you without recourse in terrain without marked trails.

The Ten Essentials

Navigation (map + compass + GPS), headlamp with spare batteries, sun protection (sunscreen + sunglasses), first aid kit (from the Safety tab), knife or multi-tool, fire starting (lighter + waterproof matches + fire starter), emergency shelter (bivy bag or emergency blanket), food (extra day's worth beyond planned), water (2L minimum + purification method), clothing (insulating layer + waterproof layer regardless of forecast).

Water in the Field

  • Sources: The Dinaric mountains produce abundant clear cold water from springs and streams. The visual clarity of mountain water is not a reliable indicator of safety — Giardia and other protozoa are invisible and produce significant illness 1–2 weeks after consumption.
  • Treatment options: Boiling: 1 minute at rolling boil kills all pathogens. Reliable, requires fuel, changes taste slightly. Chemical treatment (iodine or chlorine tablets): Cheap, lightweight, requires 30 minutes contact time, less effective against Cryptosporidium. Filtration (Sawyer Squeeze or similar): Removes bacteria and protozoa without waiting time or taste change. The Sawyer Squeeze (available online, 40–60 EUR) is the best balance of weight, cost, and effectiveness for Balkan mountain use.
  • Mountain huts (planinski domovi): The Bosnian mountain hut system — maintained by the PD Sarajevo and affiliated clubs — provides reliable water, shelter, and cooked food at many of the major summits and trails. Overnighting at a mountain hut before the first wild camping experience is the correct progression for beginners.

Fire

  • Legal position in BiH: Open fires in forests are prohibited during fire risk season (typically May–October). Check the Civil Protection authority's current fire risk status. Outside fire risk season, fires are permitted in designated areas at mountain huts and in cleared spots away from vegetation. Never light a fire in dry conditions regardless of the season — the Bosnian forests are not managed for fire resistance.
  • The fire triangle: Oxygen (present), heat (your ignition), fuel (tinder, kindling, fuel wood in that order). Tinder ignites at low temperature (dry grass, birch bark, char cloth). Kindling sustains the tinder flame long enough to ignite fuel wood. Fuel wood produces the lasting fire. Skipping any stage produces repeated failures. Start small and build.
  • Leave no trace: If a fire was lit, the ash should be scattered and the fire site should be indistinguishable from the surrounding ground when you leave. The principle applies to all camping: the site should show no evidence of occupation upon departure.

Weather & Safety in Bosnian Mountains

  • Afternoon thunderstorms: Summer afternoons in the Bosnian mountains produce violent, rapid-onset thunderstorms. The Weather tab covers this for the city — in the mountains, the same pattern arrives faster and with less warning. Plan to be below the treeline or at a hut by 14:00 during summer months. The storm typically passes within 1–2 hours.
  • Hypothermia risk: The combination of physical exertion, wet conditions, and wind produces hypothermia in temperatures as high as 10°C. A person who is wet, cold, and tired is hypothermic before they feel hypothermic — the first symptom is impaired judgment, which prevents self-assessment. The intervention: stop, change into dry insulation, eat calories, shelter from wind. The STOP principle: Stop, Think, Observe, Plan. Panic is more dangerous than the conditions that prompted it.
  • Tell someone your plan. Before any mountain excursion, tell a specific person: where you are going, which route, when you expect to return, and what to do if you have not returned by a specific time. Mountain rescue in BiH is conducted by volunteer mountain rescue services (GSS) reachable via 112. This costs nothing and represents the most important safety measure available.

Sarajevo Accessible Mountains

🏔️ Bjelašnica (2,067m)

45 minutes from the city. 1984 Winter Olympics venue. The most accessible serious mountain from Sarajevo. The Bjelašnica plateau is lunar in character — wide open grassland above the treeline, exposed to weather. A mountain hut at the summit (Planinski dom Bjelašnica). The approach from Han Bila or Umoljani villages is clearly marked. Overnight at the summit hut in October during the mushroom season is among the finest experiences available within an hour of Sarajevo.

🌲 Sutjeska National Park

3 hours from Sarajevo. Perucica — one of the last primeval forests in Europe, untouched by logging for millennia. Trnovacko jezero (glacial lake) accessible via a 5-6 hour hike from Tjentiste. The Zelengora mountain range. Bosnia's most significant wilderness. A 2-3 day trip rather than a day excursion. The Sutjeska river canyon is one of the most extraordinary natural landscapes in the Balkans.

⛰️ Trebević (1,629m)

The home mountain of Sarajevo — accessible by cable car or a 90-minute walk from the city centre. The marked trail network, the disused bobsled track, the views of the city. Not remote enough for wild camping but the ideal starting point for developing trail competence and navigation practice before longer expeditions.

The mountain does not care about your fitness level, your equipment quality, or your intentions. It responds only to your preparation, your judgment, and your willingness to turn back. The man who returns safely from a shorter trip to which he was equal is more competent than the man who reaches a summit he should not have attempted. Know the difference.— Von Lycaon
Sound shapes the mind that produces it. I am deliberate about what I allow to occupy the auditory environment during different states of work and rest — not because music is decoration, but because it is a direct input into focus, mood, and energy. Each context demands something different. What follows is not merely a list of playlists: it is an explanation of why each serves the purpose it does, and what to listen for within it.— Von Lycaon

The Principle of Intentional Listening

Most people treat music as ambience — something playing in the background that was never consciously chosen. This is a wasted resource. Music affects heart rate, cortisol levels, dopamine release, and the rate at which the prefrontal cortex processes information. Understanding what each type of music does physiologically allows you to deploy it with intention rather than habit.

The most important rule: music with lyrics competes directly with language-processing tasks. Reading, writing, analysing, and coding all use the same neural pathways as verbal processing. Lyrical music fought for in that space produces measurably worse output on language tasks than instrumental music or silence. Save lyrical music for physical tasks, commuting, and chores — where the verbal processing centre is not occupied by anything else.

Volume Principle

Low enough to stop noticing it is playing — that is the correct volume for focus work. Loud enough to feel it in the body — that is the correct volume for physical tasks. The transition between these two settings is the transition between mental and physical engagement.

Lyrical vs Instrumental

Instrumental for: deep work, reading, writing, analysis. Lyrical for: training, chores, cleaning, commuting, cooking. The rule is simple because the neuroscience is simple: two verbal streams cannot fully occupy the same channel simultaneously.

Tempo & Energy

BPM (beats per minute) influences heart rate and therefore arousal state. 60–80 BPM for focused calm work. 100–130 BPM for sustained physical effort. 130–160+ BPM for high-intensity training. Match the tempo to the physiological state you need, not the one you are already in.

Spotify Settings

Enable: Crossfade (5 seconds — prevents jarring transitions between tracks). Audio quality: Very High. Disable: Autoplay after playlist ends — it will add algorithmically chosen tracks that break your curated context. Let silence follow a finished playlist rather than an algorithm filling it.

Deep Focus — Work & Study

The goal during focused analytical work is to occupy just enough of the auditory cortex to suppress environmental distraction, without competing with the language centres needed for reading and writing. Ambient, textural, and classical instrumental music achieves this. The brain habituates to it within minutes, after which it functions as a cognitive shield rather than an additional input.

🎹 Hans Zimmer — Study & Work

Cinematic orchestral without lyrics. Interstellar, Inception, and Gladiator soundtracks contain extended tracks at 60–80 BPM ideal for sustained analytical work. The harmonic language is rich enough to be interesting but never demanding enough to pull attention away from the task. Search: "Hans Zimmer study music" on Spotify.

🎵 Lofi Hip-Hop

The genre was engineered for exactly this use case. Slow BPM (70–90), no lyrics, gentle percussion, warm analogue textures. "Lofi Girl" on Spotify and YouTube is the canonical source. The slight imperfections in the production — the vinyl crackle, the slightly off-beat hi-hats — prevent the brain from fully habituating to silence, which keeps alertness marginally elevated without stimulation.

🎻 Chopin Nocturnes / Bach Cello Suites

For those who find modern ambient music texturally thin: Chopin's Nocturnes (Op.9, Op.15, Op.27) are among the most focus-compatible pieces ever written — melodically present but emotionally unhurried. Bach's Cello Suites in G Major are similarly calibrated. Both at low volume. Both on every streaming platform.

🌧️ Rain & Brown Noise

Not music — but a legitimate auditory focus tool. Brown noise (deeper than white noise) has the strongest research support for masking distraction and improving sustained attention. Rain sounds achieve a similar effect through natural familiarity. "Rain on Window" or "Brown Noise" on Spotify or YouTube. For open-plan offices or environments with irregular noise patterns.

🎷 Jazz — Miles Davis / Coltrane

Kind of Blue by Miles Davis (1959) is the most-streamed jazz album for a reason: it operates at exactly the threshold of interesting-without-demanding. For focused work, Kind of Blue, Bill Evans' Waltz for Debby, or any Coltrane ballads album. Avoid bebop (too rhythmically complex, too demanding) — the slow modal jazz is the target.

🏛️ Dark Academia Playlist

Spotify genre tag "Dark Academia" — a curated aesthetic combining classical piano, string quartets, and ambient orchestral. Consistent with the working environment described in the Windows tab. Search "dark academia study music" for hundreds of community-curated playlists. Quality varies — find one with 50k+ followers and commit to it.

Training & Workout

Physical training requires the opposite of focus music. High BPM, strong rhythmic drive, lyrics that are aggressive enough to provide motivational signal without demanding active listening. The goal is to raise physiological arousal and maintain it through the session. What you play during a workout influences actual performance output — not marginally.

⚡ Hybrid Trap / Dark Trap

110–140 BPM, heavy bass, cinematic build-ups. Artists: Travis Scott, Kanye West (Yeezus, DONDA), Playboi Carti. The dark, driving aesthetic fits the controlled intensity of a bodyweight session better than pop-oriented workout playlists. Search "dark trap workout" on Spotify.

🥊 Hip-Hop — High Energy

Kendrick Lamar (DAMN., To Pimp a Butterfly), J. Cole, Jay-Z. Lyrical enough to occupy attention during rest periods, rhythmically driven enough to push through working sets. Not the highest BPM option — but provides the most sustained motivational content over a full 60-minute session.

🔊 Electronic — Techno & Drum & Bass

For maximum output on conditioning work, push-up ladders, or jump squats: 140–175 BPM techno or drum & bass. Artists: Chase & Status, Noisia, Aphex Twin (Selected Ambient Works II excluded — that is focus music). The relentless mechanical rhythm removes the option of slowing down. Search "workout drum and bass" or "techno workout".

🎸 Rock & Metal — Controlled Aggression

For those who find electronic music texturally thin during heavy effort: Metallica (Black Album, Master of Puppets), Queens of the Stone Age, Rage Against the Machine. Strong beat, clear structure, aggressive energy. The guitar tone provides a physical quality that electronic production sometimes lacks.

Chores, Cleaning & Cooking

Physical domestic tasks — cleaning, tidying, cooking, laundry — are uniquely suited to music that would be too distracting for mental work. Lyrics are fine. High energy is welcome. This is one of the few contexts where the phone should be played loud and the task approached with something closer to performance than obligation. A well-selected playlist converts 45 minutes of cleaning into 45 minutes of something genuinely enjoyable.

🫧 Upbeat Pop & Funk — Cleaning Tempo

100–120 BPM, rhythmically clear, melodically accessible. The tempo naturally synchronises with movement — wiping surfaces, mopping, vacuuming all align with a 4/4 beat at this range. Artists: Bruno Mars (24K Magic album is the canonical cleaning album), Dua Lipa, The Weeknd (Starboy). Search "cleaning playlist" or "feel good pop" on Spotify — the community-curated options in this genre are consistently good.

🕺 Disco & 70s Funk

Earth, Wind & Fire, Chic, Donna Summer, Michael Jackson (Off the Wall specifically — more funk than pop). The consistent rhythmic pulse of disco was designed for physical movement and translates perfectly to domestic tasks. The production is warm, the tempo is consistent, and it is impossible to be genuinely irritable while cleaning to "September" by Earth, Wind & Fire. This is a statement of physiological fact.

🎤 R&B — Smooth & Soulful

For slower, more meditative domestic tasks — hand-washing dishes, folding laundry, preparing a long-cook meal. Frank Ocean (Blonde, Channel Orange), SZA, H.E.R., Daniel Caesar. Lower BPM than the cleaning category (80–100), more textured, more lyrical. Matches the pace of tasks that benefit from calm attention rather than energised efficiency.

🍳 Cooking — Jazz Café

When cooking a proper meal — one that requires attention, timing, and some craft — aggressive music fragments concentration in the kitchen the same way it does at the desk. Jazz café style: Norah Jones, Diana Krall, Chet Baker, Antonio Carlos Jobim (bossa nova). Warm, melodic, rhythmically present but never demanding. The kind of music that makes the kitchen feel like somewhere worth spending time. Search "jazz café" or "cooking jazz" on Spotify.

🎵 Afrobeats & Latin

Burna Boy, WizKid, Bad Bunny, J Balvin. Rhythmically complex, physically engaging, culturally varied — for those who want their cleaning playlist to feel less like a Western gym soundtrack. The polyrhythmic structure keeps the brain marginally more engaged than four-on-the-floor pop, which prevents the boredom that can emerge during longer cleaning sessions.

📻 Podcasts During Repetitive Tasks

Not music — but the correct tool for certain domestic tasks. Ironing, folding laundry, peeling vegetables, and any task that is purely mechanical benefits from an engaging podcast rather than music. The task does not require auditory attention management. Use the time productively. Recommendations in the YouTube tab; the same content applies to audio-only podcast format via Pocket Casts or Spotify.

Morning & Evening Wind-Down

🌅 Morning — Slow Rise

The first 30 minutes of the day are described in the Daily Upkeep tab. If music is used during this window, it should not be stimulating — the point is a gradual increase in arousal, not an abrupt spike. Acoustic guitar, gentle piano, soft jazz. Artists: Nils Frahm, Ólafur Arnalds, Acoustic Sunrise playlists. 60–70 BPM. Volume low. Let the body lead.

🌙 Evening — Wind-Down

After 21:00, the auditory environment should not compete with the process of cortisol reduction and melatonin onset. Ambient electronic: Brian Eno (Ambient 1: Music for Airports), Max Richter (Sleep — an 8-hour composition literally designed for this purpose), Stars of the Lid. Or silence. Silence is not the absence of music — it is a deliberate choice. Both are valid. Aggressive or lyrical music after 21:00 extends the time to sleep onset measurably.

☕ Weekend Morning — Coffee & Reading

The weekend morning described in the Daily Eating tab — coffee made properly, something to read. The music for this context: acoustic jazz, bossa nova, or classical piano. Bill Evans (Waltz for Debby), João Gilberto, Erik Satie (Gymnopédies). Nothing that demands attention. Everything that makes the room feel like a place worth inhabiting slowly.

Commute & Travel

🚃 Tram — Sarajevo Commute

The commute is transition time — between home and work, or work and home. Music here should be deliberately chosen to manage the transition. Into work: building energy, moderate BPM, something motivating. Into home: decompressing, lower BPM, something that signals the day is ending. Maintaining the same playlist for both commutes forfeits the management of two psychologically important transitions per day.

🎧 Into Work — Building Energy

80–110 BPM, forward momentum. Suggestions: Daft Punk (Random Access Memories), Pharrell Williams, Childish Gambino (Awaken, My Love!). Sets the tone for a capable, composed arrival. Not aggressive — composed. The difference matters in the context of an office morning.

🏠 Into Home — Decompression

After the work day: something that requires nothing. Frank Ocean, Daniel Caesar, a jazz playlist, or an ambient album. The commute home should mentally close the work context before the front door opens. Arriving home still mentally at work is a friction cost paid by everyone in the household, not just you.

Playlist Starting Points — Spotify Search Terms

  • Focus work: "lofi hip hop study", "Hans Zimmer work music", "dark academia study", "Bach focus", "brown noise"
  • Training: "dark trap workout", "hip hop training", "drum and bass workout", "metal gym"
  • Cleaning & chores: "cleaning playlist", "Bruno Mars playlist", "70s funk disco", "feel good pop"
  • Cooking: "jazz café cooking", "bossa nova kitchen", "acoustic cooking playlist"
  • Morning: "acoustic morning", "Nils Frahm morning", "piano morning coffee"
  • Evening: "sleep ambient", "Max Richter sleep", "wind down playlist", "Brian Eno ambient"
  • Commute in: "morning commute energy", "Daft Punk playlist", "feel good morning"
  • Commute home: "evening commute chill", "Frank Ocean playlist", "R&B wind down"
I do not treat silence as failure. There are hours in the day — the first thirty minutes, deep reading sessions, certain evenings — where silence is the correct choice and any music would be an intrusion. The discipline is not filling every moment with sound. It is choosing, with intention, when sound serves the moment and when it does not.— Von Lycaon
The phone is the most personal tool you carry. It is also, if left unmanaged, the most persistent source of fragmented attention in your life. I apply to it the same principle I apply to the desk: nothing present that does not earn its place. The apps you keep, the notifications you permit, and the way you organise the device are a direct reflection of how seriously you take your own attention.— Von Lycaon

The Notification Audit — First Principles

Before discussing any specific app, the most important intervention available on a modern smartphone: turn off every notification, then deliberately re-enable only the ones that genuinely require immediate response. Most people do the reverse — accept all notifications by default and disable the ones that become intolerable. The result is a device that interrupts constantly.

  • Immediate response required (allow): Phone calls from people who matter. Direct messages from close contacts. Calendar alerts for imminent events. That is likely a complete list for most people.
  • Batched — check on your schedule (silent / badge only): Email. Messaging apps. Social media. News. These do not require interruption — they reward scheduled attention.
  • Never (disable entirely): Every app that delivers content rather than communication. YouTube, Instagram, LinkedIn, browser, weather. These apps have no interest in your time. You decide when to engage with them.
  • On Android: Settings → Notifications → App notifications. Review each app individually. Twenty minutes once, daily returns indefinitely.

Home Screen Architecture

The One-Screen Rule

Everything needed daily on one screen. No folders on the home screen. No second or third page. If an app cannot earn a visible place, it opens from the app drawer. The discipline is in the curation.

Dock — Four Apps Maximum

Phone. Messaging (WhatsApp). Browser. One productivity app (calendar or notes). These four service every urgent need. Anything else can wait the two seconds to open the app drawer.

Wallpaper Standard

Dark, atmospheric, non-busy. Same aesthetic direction as the Windows desktop. A cluttered phone wallpaper buried under widgets produces the same cognitive noise as a cluttered desk. Nothing that contains text or busy colour.

Niagara Launcher

The most elegant minimal Android launcher. A single scrollable alphabetical list on one side, recent apps on the other. No grid, no icon clutter. Free on Play Store. Transforms the phone into a quieter, more intentional instrument.

Home Screen Setup (Step by Step)

  1. Remove all apps from the home screen. Long-press → Remove from Home. Start from zero.
  2. Set a clean dark wallpaper. Wallhaven.cc or Unsplash — same sources as the Windows desktop tab.
  3. Install Niagara Launcher from Play Store if desired. Set as default launcher in Settings → Apps → Default apps.
  4. Place only genuinely daily-use apps. If you did not open it yesterday, it does not belong on the home screen today.
  5. Perform the notification audit before returning to normal use. The screen is now clean — keep the notifications equally so.

Productivity & Organisation

📅 Google Calendar

The standard. Every commitment, appointment, and deadline in one place, synced across devices. Use colour coding: red for deadlines, green for personal, blue for work. If it is not in the calendar, it does not exist as a commitment.

✅ Todoist

Task management with natural language input — type "Submit report Friday 3pm" and it parses automatically. Free tier is fully functional. The GTD method implemented cleanly. Available globally, no regional restrictions.

📝 Obsidian Mobile

The same note system as the desktop, available on the phone. Local-first — notes stored as plain Markdown files. Sync via a cloud folder of your choosing. Free. Your notes, references, and journal always within reach.

📋 Notion

For those who prefer a visual, database-driven organisation system. Project tracking, reading lists, habit trackers, and reference databases in one place. Free tier covers personal use entirely. Works offline with cached content.

⏱️ Toggl Track

Time tracking for work and personal projects. Understand where your time actually goes — not where you believe it goes. Free tier. Syncs with desktop. The act of tracking time changes how deliberately you spend it.

📖 Moon+ Reader

The definitive Android e-book reader. Supports EPUB, PDF, MOBI, and more. Customisable fonts, margins, background colour (sepia or dark mode). Import books directly from a file manager. Free with optional one-time premium.

Security & Privacy

🔑 Bitwarden

Open-source password manager — mobile companion to the desktop app. Autofills credentials in every app and browser. The password you cannot remember is the password you will not reuse on twenty sites. The only acceptable password strategy is a unique, strong, generated password for every account.

🔒 Proton VPN

Free tier genuinely functional for everyday use. Swiss-based, no-logs policy. Essential on public Wi-Fi in cafes and the SCC food court — never use public networks for anything requiring credentials without a VPN active.

🛡️ Proton Mail

End-to-end encrypted email. Free tier provides a @proton.me address with 1GB storage. For any correspondence you consider private — medical, financial, legal, or personal. Pairs with Proton VPN for a complete privacy stack.

🔐 Google Authenticator / Aegis

Two-factor authentication app. Aegis (free, open-source) is the superior option — it allows encrypted backups of your 2FA codes, which Google Authenticator does not. Enable 2FA on every account that offers it: email, banking, social media. Without it, a password alone is insufficient.

📵 Digital Wellbeing

Built into Android. Settings → Digital Wellbeing. Set daily time limits on high-distraction apps (Instagram, YouTube, TikTok). Not a permanent ban — a deliberate boundary. When the limit is reached, the app greyed out is a friction point that interrupts the automatic scroll habit before it consumes another hour.

Health & Fitness

💪 Strong

Workout logging app. Log every session: exercises, sets, reps, rest times. Progress graphs over weeks and months reveal what is working and what is not. The bodyweight programme from the Workout tab tracked here becomes a measurable record rather than a vague impression. Free tier sufficient for most users.

🥗 Cronometer

Nutrition and macro tracking. More accurate than MyFitnessPal — Cronometer uses USDA and verified data sources rather than user-submitted entries which are frequently incorrect. Track protein intake daily until hitting the target becomes automatic — then stop tracking. The goal is calibration, not permanent surveillance.

😴 Sleep as Android

Sleep cycle tracking using the phone's accelerometer. Wakes you during light sleep within a configurable window (e.g. 6:45–7:15am), avoiding the disorientation of waking from deep sleep mid-cycle. Smart alarm genuinely works. More actionable sleep data than most dedicated sleep trackers.

🧘 Insight Timer

Free meditation and breathing timer. No subscription required for guided breathing, timers, and a substantial free library of guided sessions. For the morning two minutes of silence and the pre-sleep wind-down protocol from the Mind & Rest tab.

💧 Hydro Coach

Hydration tracking with reminders calibrated to your bodyweight and activity level. Dehydration of 2% reduces cognitive performance measurably. The reminder is the value — most people do not drink enough water because they do not notice thirst until it becomes significant.

Media & Entertainment

🎵 Spotify

Fully functional in Bosnia. Download playlists for offline use — the tram signal in the SCC tunnel is unreliable. Premium is approximately 5.99 EUR/month at Balkans regional pricing. The single best value entertainment subscription available.

🎙️ Pocket Casts

The definitive podcast app. Trim silence, variable playback speed (1.3x is the sweet spot — faster comprehension, not yet chipmunk), chapter support, cross-device sync. One-time purchase. For the commute, the walk, the gym — audio content during otherwise idle time.

📺 YouTube ReVanced

YouTube without advertisements, with background playback (audio continues when the screen is off), and picture-in-picture. Not on the Play Store — install via the ReVanced Manager app (available at revanced.app). Legal in Bosnia Herzegovina. Transforms YouTube from an attention trap into a controlled media tool.

📷 Adobe Lightroom Mobile

Free tier covers everything a non-professional needs. Colour and exposure control with actual precision rather than the aggressive preset-based editing of Instagram filters. If you are going to document your city and your life, do it with some intention.

📻 Spotify Podcasts / Overcast

If Pocket Casts feels like more than needed — Spotify now hosts most major podcasts natively, and Overcast (iOS) or its Android equivalents are clean free alternatives. The specific app matters less than the habit of structured audio learning during commutes.

Utilities & Tools

🗺️ Google Maps + Maps.me

Google Maps for real-time navigation and business search. Maps.me downloads full offline maps of Bosnia — essential in areas with poor data coverage or when data roaming is expensive. Keep both. The offline map is specifically for when you need it and the network is absent.

🌤️ Weather Underground

More granular local weather than the default app. Sarajevo's valley geography creates microclimatic variation that large-scale services miss. Knowing whether rain arrives in the next two hours is a practical question in a city prone to afternoon storms.

💸 Splitwise

Shared expense tracking. For group travel, shared dinners, flatmate expenses. Eliminates the awkward mental accounting that persists after every shared occasion. Free tier covers all practical use cases.

🔦 Solid Explorer

The best Android file manager. Two-panel view, FTP/SFTP/cloud support, root access if needed. For the rare occasions when the phone's file system needs direct interaction — moving downloaded files, accessing device storage, connecting to a NAS. One-time purchase, worth it.

📡 WiFi Analyzer

Scans and maps nearby WiFi networks, signal strength, and channel congestion. Useful for diagnosing slow home internet (if your router and a neighbour's are on the same channel, congestion slows both). Free. Takes 30 seconds to diagnose what a call to the ISP takes 30 minutes to not solve.

🖨️ Adobe Scan

Scan documents with the phone camera — auto-detects edges, corrects perspective, exports as PDF. For receipts, contracts, and any paper document that needs to exist digitally. Free. In combination with Proton Drive or Google Drive, eliminates the need to own a physical scanner for 95% of use cases.

Android Customisation — Beyond the Launcher

Always-On Display & Lock Screen

  • Always-On Display: Settings → Lock screen → Always On Display: On. Shows time, date, and notification icons without waking the full screen. Eliminates the habit of unlocking the phone to check the time — which reliably leads to checking notifications, which leads to thirty minutes disappearing.
  • Lock screen shortcuts: Remove social media shortcuts from the lock screen. Replace with Torch and Camera if your device allows it — or remove shortcuts entirely. The lock screen should provide information, not temptation.
  • Lock screen widgets: On supported Android versions (14+), add a minimal clock widget and battery percentage only. Nothing that invites scrolling before the day has properly begun.

System Font & Display

  • Font size: Settings → Display → Font size and style. Increase by one step from default. Eye strain over a full day of reading on a small screen is real — larger text is not vanity, it is visual economy.
  • Display size: Settings → Display → Display size. Slightly smaller than default on large-screen phones creates a cleaner, less crowded interface. Test at one step below default.
  • System font (Samsung / MIUI): Settings → Display → Font style. Samsung devices allow font changes without third-party apps. Install "Samsung One UI Sans" or "One UI 6" font styles for a more refined interface. On stock Android, this requires a launcher that supports font changes (Niagara does not — Lawnchair 2 does).
  • Colour profile: Settings → Display → Screen mode → Natural or AMOLED Cinema. Avoid the default "Vivid" — it over-saturates colours in a way that looks artificial in direct comparison with physical objects. Natural is accurate; AMOLED Cinema is a slight enhancement without distortion.

Battery & Performance

  • Adaptive Battery: On. Settings → Battery → Adaptive Battery. Learns your usage patterns and restricts background activity for rarely used apps. More effective than manually restricting individual apps because it operates on actual usage data.
  • Background app refresh: Settings → Battery → Background usage limits → Restrict background usage for every app that does not need to push real-time data. Social media apps do not need background access — they can refresh when you open them.
  • Charge limit at 80% (if device supports it): Settings → Battery → Protect Battery. Stops charging at 80% to extend long-term battery health. The full 80–100% range degrades lithium-ion cells significantly faster than the 20–80% range. On a phone you intend to keep for 3+ years, this is meaningful.
  • Dark mode system-wide: Settings → Display → Dark: On. On AMOLED screens (most Samsung, most flagship Android), dark mode meaningfully extends battery life because black pixels on AMOLED are literally powered off. Not merely dim — off.

Quick Settings Panel

  • Audit and reorder: Swipe down twice → Edit (pencil icon). Remove tiles for: NFC (unless you use it daily), Nearby Share, Screen recorder, Samsung Pay shortcut. Add: Hotspot (one tap to share data), Screen recorder if used, VPN toggle.
  • Useful shortcut order: WiFi, Mobile Data, Bluetooth, Torch, DND (Do Not Disturb), Hotspot, VPN, Screen brightness (auto). Everything else is accessible via Settings and does not require a quick tile.
  • Do Not Disturb schedule: Settings → Sounds → Do Not Disturb → Schedules. Set DND automatically from 22:00 to 07:30. All notifications silenced during sleep hours without needing to remember to enable it each night. Allow exceptions: calls from Starred contacts (family) only.

Digital Organisation — The Phone File System

  • Photos: reviewed weekly. Five minutes Sunday — remove duplicates, temporary screenshots, blurred photos. A library of 40,000 images contains no more accessible memories than 4,000 curated ones. The curation is the act of deciding what mattered.
  • Downloads folder: cleared monthly. A transit point, not storage. Every file is either moved to its correct location or deleted. Nothing stays in Downloads beyond one week.
  • Contacts: maintained annually. Remove entries with no name, no longer relevant, or duplicated. A contact list that cannot be navigated is not a contact list — it is a list.
  • WhatsApp: archived, not buried. Archive concluded group chats. Mute ongoing ones that do not require real-time attention. The notification noise from irrelevant groups is the most common source of phone overload.
  • App permissions: audited quarterly. Settings → Privacy → Permission manager. Which apps have location always? Microphone? Camera? An app requiring location always has exceeded its brief unless it is a navigation tool.

The Phone-Free Zones

The phone is a tool. A tool used constantly and without intention becomes a habit. A habit that structures the day around itself rather than the life it is supposed to serve has exceeded its brief. I enforce three phone-free zones — not as asceticism, but as a practical defence of the attention that produces everything else in this codex.— Von Lycaon
  • The first 30 minutes of the day. The tone of the morning belongs to you, not to notifications from people who went to bed later than you.
  • Mealtimes. Alone or with others. The food is better tasted, the company is better kept, and digestion is literally improved by the parasympathetic engagement that scrolling actively prevents.
  • One hour before sleep. The screen-free hour before bed is the single highest-return sleep intervention available at zero cost. Already covered in the Mind & Rest tab — applies here equally.
An attendant who cannot produce what is needed, precisely when it is needed, has not prepared adequately. The contents of one's daily bag are a direct measure of one's foresight. I carry nothing unnecessary. Nothing necessary is missing. Every item in the bag has earned its place through utility — not sentimentality, not habit, and not the optimistic assumption that I might need it someday.— Von Lycaon

The Bag Itself

Size

15–20 litres. Enough to carry what matters. Small enough to remain unobtrusive and to fit under an aircraft seat. A bag that requires its own announcement upon entering a room is too large.

Material

Waxed canvas in black or charcoal (weathers well, looks better with age), or ballistic nylon (lighter, more water-resistant). No glossy materials. Nothing that requires special care. In Sarajevo's wet winters, water resistance is a practical requirement, not a luxury.

Organisation

At minimum: one padded laptop sleeve, one structured front pocket, and a main compartment. A bag where everything lives in one undivided space means finding anything requires removing everything. This happens, on average, at the worst possible moment.

Where to find in Sarajevo: Koton (SCC) and Reserved (SCC) carry minimal backpacks at 50–100 BAM. For a more deliberate choice: Kupi.ba carries Herschel, Eastpak, and similar brands with reliable delivery.

The Contents — Each Item Justified

Laptop & Charger

In its sleeve, charger coiled with a velcro strap. A coiled cable is re-deployable in twenty seconds. A tangled cable is a test of patience at a critical moment. Keep the power adapter in the bag permanently — not removed at home and forgotten in the morning.

Power Bank — 10,000 mAh

Anker or Xiaomi (both at m:tel stores, SCC electronics, or Extreme computers on Ferhadija). Not 5,000 mAh — that charges a phone once. 10,000 mAh covers a phone and earphones and still has reserve. The day you need it and don't have it, you will carry one forever after.

Notebook A5 + Pen

Leuchtturm1917 or Moleskine — both at Algoritam (BBI) and Buybook (Radićeva). A Pilot G2 or Stabilo pen, both stocked at the same stores. The specific notebook matters less than the habit: ideas, commitments, and observations written down are retrieved. Those trusted to memory alone are frequently lost.

Earphones / Headphones

In a hard case. The case is the rule, not the suggestion — earphones found loose in a bag are earphones found tangled. If you use wireless earphones, a small charging cable for them lives in the case permanently. Never assume they are charged.

Water Bottle — 500ml Insulated

Intersport (SCC) carries Nalgene and several no-brand insulated bottles. Hydration throughout the day maintains focus better than any supplement. Fill it before leaving the house. It is the first thing packed, not an afterthought.

USB-C Hub / Adapters

A compact hub covering USB-A, HDMI, and SD card slots. Modern laptops increasingly have only USB-C ports; a hub carried consistently means you are never the person asking to borrow a cable in a meeting. Available at Extreme computers on Ferhadija or SCC electronics stores.

Medications Pouch

Paracetamol, ibuprofen, a single antihistamine tablet, and two plasters. A small zip-seal bag. These together weigh almost nothing and remove the dependence on others when a headache or minor injury occurs. All available at dm or any apoteka throughout Sarajevo.

Grooming Pouch

Labello Classic (dm), Colgate Mints or similar (dm), a travel-size deodorant (Nivea Men Sensitive, dm), a folding comb. This pouch is restocked weekly. Running out of lip balm at 2pm on a Sarajevo January day is an entirely preventable inconvenience.

Slim Card Holder / Wallet

Not a billfold stuffed with six months of receipts. Two cards, identification, and cash. What is not in the wallet cannot be lost. Keep a digital photograph of essential documents stored offline on your phone as backup.

Reusable Bag — Folded

Occupies no meaningful space folded flat. Eliminates the need for a plastic bag at any impromptu purchase. The small signal of producing a fabric bag is a signal of forethought. These details accumulate.

A Book or E-Reader

For commutes, waiting rooms, and any idle moment. The person with a book is never bored and never without something better to do than scroll. Kindle Paperwhite available via Kupi.ba. Physical books from Buybook or Algoritam.

Compact Umbrella

A fully collapsible umbrella. Sarajevo receives meaningful rainfall and sudden afternoon storms in spring and autumn. The person who carries a compact umbrella is the person who arrives at every meeting dry. Available at dm, Konzum, and most supermarkets. Keep one in the bag permanently — remove it only when forecasts are certain.

Packing Rules

  • Pack the bag the evening prior. Never in the morning. The morning has enough friction without adding the mental load of assembling the bag under time pressure.
  • Assign everything a fixed place. The laptop is always in the sleeve. The medications are always in the front right pocket. The notebook is always in the front left. Fixed placement means retrieval without searching.
  • The bag returns empty of temporary items, stocked with permanent ones. Receipts, used tissues, and empty packaging are removed each evening. The permanent items — medications, grooming pouch, adapters — are always there and never removed.
  • Weigh it annually. The bag's weight creeps upward over time as items are added and never removed. Once per year, empty it completely. Re-pack only what has been used in the past month. Everything else is cargo.
I do not discourage leisure. I discourage thoughtless leisure. Gaming, approached with the same intentionality I bring to other domains, is a legitimate and enriching pursuit. It exercises the mind, trains pattern recognition, builds tolerance for failure — and when played with others, demands the same communication and reliability I apply professionally. The game reflects the player. Play accordingly.— Von Lycaon

The Gaming Mindset

🧠

Understand Before You Compete

Before entering ranked or competitive modes, understand the game's mechanics completely. This applies in Zenless Zone Zero, in strategy games, and in any competitive multiplayer. Rushing to compete before understanding the system produces frustration, not growth. Spend the first sessions in the open world, tutorial, or casual modes. Observe the patterns. Then compete.

📉

Mine Losses for Data

After a loss: one minute of reflection. What did I fail to anticipate? What decision was wrong and at what moment? What will I do differently? This review does not need to be elaborate — the discipline of asking the question after each loss accelerates improvement faster than double the play time without reflection.

⏰

Decide Session Length Before Starting

Set a time before you sit down. Not "one more game" — a specific clock time at which the session ends. An open-ended session is how hours disappear. A scheduled session ends when you chose it to, not when the game's reward loops have extended the experience beyond what was healthy. The game's design specifically resists your stopping. Decide before it has that influence.

🤝

Be a Good Teammate

Communicate clearly. Never flame. Credit good plays from allies explicitly. In voice chat: concise callouts, no commentary on poor plays unless constructive. The reputation built in online spaces is still a reputation. Those who encounter you there form impressions. This matters more than it appears to.

Zenless Zone Zero — Lycaon's Own Game

Since you have drawn your inspiration from Von Lycaon, it is appropriate to address the game he inhabits directly. ZZZ is a character-action RPG set in New Eridu. The combat is timing-based with a strong emphasis on team synergy. Lycaon himself excels in stagger builds — exploiting daze mechanics to create openings for the team's primary damage dealer.

Understanding Hollows

The Hollow exploration system involves navigating tile-based maps with limited energy. Prioritise the path that collects TV-card resources efficiently before committing to the boss encounter. Rushing to the boss with suboptimal resources is a repeatable failure pattern.

Team Composition

ZZZ rewards building teams around a specific element and attribute synergy. Before assigning characters to a team, identify your primary damage dealer and build the remaining two slots to amplify their specific damage type — rather than using three independently strong characters with no synergy.

The Stagger Mechanic

Building stagger/daze on enemies creates a window in which all damage dealt is amplified significantly. This window is brief. Train the reflex to switch to your primary damage dealer the moment the stagger state appears — even mid-combo. Reaction speed here is a trained habit, not an innate reflex.

Setup Standards

  • Audio calibration: In competitive games, audio directional cues (footsteps, ability sounds, environmental tells) carry significant information. Take ten minutes to calibrate your headset's equaliser — boosting frequencies in the 2–4kHz range increases footstep audibility without distorting the overall soundscape. Many gaming headsets have companion software for this. Logitech G Hub and SteelSeries Engine are both available as free downloads.
  • Mouse DPI and sensitivity: Lower DPI with higher in-game sensitivity produces more granular control than high DPI with low in-game sensitivity, because lower DPI uses the physical sensor more efficiently. Test your current DPI at mouseaccuracy.com. Find the highest sensitivity at which you can reliably land precise crosshair placements. Drop it by 20%. Play at that level for two weeks before evaluating.
  • Monitor refresh rate — enabled: Right-click Desktop → Display settings → Advanced display → Choose a refresh rate → Select the highest available. Many people own 144Hz monitors running at 60Hz because this was never changed from the default.
  • Posture for long sessions: Monitor at eye level. Chair adjusted so thighs are parallel to the floor. Keyboard at elbow height. The wrist should not be bent upward during mouse use — consider a wrist rest if this is unavoidable. Two minutes of shoulder and neck stretching per hour of play.
  • Eye care: The 20-20-20 rule. Every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet (6 metres) away for 20 seconds. This exercises the focal length muscles of the eye. In a gaming session of two hours, this means three brief interruptions. Set a phone timer for the first few weeks until it becomes automatic.

Genres Worth Your Time

Strategy (RTS / TBS)+

Strategy games train resource management, forward planning, and the understanding that early decisions compound into late-game positions. The StarCraft, Civilization, and Total War series are the canonical examples. More accessible entry points: Into the Breach (a small, brilliant puzzle-strategy game), or Crusader Kings III if you wish to spend 300 hours understanding how alliances, diplomacy, and long-term planning interact in a dynamic system. The skills these games build — patience, multi-variable thinking, comfort with incomplete information — transfer more directly to professional work than most training programmes.

Role-Playing Games+

At their best, RPGs offer narratives of genuine literary quality and moral questions worth examining outside the game. Disco Elysium is the apex of this — a detective RPG with writing that has been compared favourably to serious literary fiction, and choices that have genuine consequences in the game's world. Baldur's Gate 3, released recently, is the closest modern equivalent of a tabletop campaign with a full party. For something more atmospheric and brief: What Remains of Edith Finch takes approximately two hours and will likely linger for years. These are not merely games — they are experiences that reward the same engagement you would bring to a good novel.

Cooperative Multiplayer+

Games that require genuine coordination and communication with others produce something beyond entertainment: they practise the exact skills that make a person valuable in team-based professional environments. Deep Rock Galactic, for example, is a cooperative shooter in which four players fill distinct roles (Scout, Driller, Engineer, Gunner) with genuinely complementary abilities. Success requires role clarity, callouts, and covering for each other's vulnerabilities — which is, precisely, what an effective professional team does. Playing this kind of game with people you know and communicating earnestly is more social-skills-building than most activities marketed as such.

Puzzle Games+

Brief, satisfying, and among the most efficient uses of a 30-minute gaming window. Return of the Obra Dinn is a deductive logic puzzle set on a ghost ship — one of the most satisfying problem-solving experiences in interactive media. The Witness is a 3D exploration-puzzle game on an island whose rules you must discover through observation, with no tutorials or instructions. Portal 2 remains the standard by which puzzle-game design is measured. Baba Is You is one of the most structurally inventive puzzle games ever made — you manipulate the rules of the game itself as game objects. Each of these builds a specific kind of systematic thinking that is genuinely applicable beyond the game.

The Balance Principle

Gaming is not unlike any other appetite — moderate and deliberate engagement is enriching. Unregulated consumption becomes the primary activity around which life is awkwardly arranged. The signals that balance has been lost: you play to avoid something rather than to enjoy something. You feel worse after playing than before. You find yourself thinking about the game during work in a way that degrades the work rather than a brief pleasant thought. You are regularly cancelling other commitments to accommodate sessions.

None of this indicates you should stop playing. It indicates you should restore the deliberate relationship with it — the scheduled session, the defined end time, the choice that it is the activity for now rather than the default option that plays until some external interruption ends it.

I play. I simply decide when I play, for how long, and why — before I begin. The game has no say in this. That is the only real distinction between a hobby and a consumption habit.— Von Lycaon, on leisure with intention
YouTube is either the most valuable free education platform ever created or a mechanism for dissolving hours without trace. The distinction is entirely in how it is used. The channels below are curated by a single criterion: does spending an hour here make you measurably more capable, informed, or skilled? Everything else is entertainment — which has its place, but not in this list.— Von Lycaon

Science & Health

🧠
Andrew Huberman
Huberman Lab · 6M+ subscribers
Stanford neuroscientist. Long-form episodes (2–3 hours) on sleep, focus, hormones, exercise science, and mental health. The most practically applicable science content available on YouTube. Every episode produces actionable protocol changes.
NeurosciencePerformanceSleep
Start with: "The Science of Making & Breaking Habits"
🔬
Kurzgesagt
22M+ subscribers
Animated science explanations on topics from immune systems to black holes. Exceptionally clear visual communication of complex concepts. The quality of research behind each video is genuinely peer-reviewed level.
ScienceBiologyPhysics
Start with: "The Most Efficient Way to Destroy the Universe"
💊
Peter Attia
The Drive · 1M+ subscribers
Longevity physician. Deep dives into metabolic health, cardiovascular disease, cancer prevention, and the Outlive framework. The companion to the book of the same name on the Reading List.
LongevityMedicineNutrition
Start with: "Zone 2 Training and Longevity"

Technology & IT

🐍
Corey Schafer
Python & software development
The clearest Python tutorial channel available. Methodical, well-paced, genuinely expert explanations. From beginner to advanced. For IT analysts building Python skills — this channel and consistent daily practice constitutes a complete self-education.
PythonSQLTutorials
Start with: "Python Tutorial for Beginners" series
📊
Alex The Analyst
Data analytics career content
Data analyst career development — SQL, Power BI, Excel, Python. Practical, portfolio-oriented tutorials. Directly applicable to an IT analyst role building toward data analytics capability.
SQLPower BIAnalytics
Start with: "Data Analyst Portfolio Project"
🔐
NetworkChuck
Networking & cybersecurity
Networking, Linux, cybersecurity, and cloud concepts. Energetic delivery over genuine technical depth. Useful for IT professionals broadening beyond their primary specialty into infrastructure and security.
NetworkingSecurityLinux
Start with: "Free CCNA course"

Finance & Economics

📈
Plain Bagel
Investing & personal finance
Clear, skeptical, evidence-based personal finance content. Anti-hype, pro-index-funds, genuinely useful for someone building investment literacy from a solid foundation. Canadian-based but universally applicable principles.
InvestingIndex fundsSkeptical
Start with: "Why You Can't Beat the Market"
💰
Andrei Jikh
Personal finance & investing
Accessible investing and financial independence content. More entertainment-oriented than Plain Bagel but the foundational principles are sound and the production quality is high. Entry-level friendly.
FIREInvesting basics
Start with: "How I'd Invest $1000 Right Now"

Philosophy & Thinking

🎭
Einzelgänger
Philosophy applied to daily life
Stoicism, Buddhism, Taoism, and existentialism applied practically. High production quality, thoughtful scripts, no academic jargon. The Stoicism Applied tab in this Codex and this channel are natural companions.
StoicismBuddhismApplied
Start with: "The Stoic Response to Criticism"
🧩
Veritasium
Physics & scientific thinking
Physics, mathematics, and scientific thinking at the intersection of counterintuitive and important. Derek Muller's background in physics education research produces videos that specifically target and correct misconceptions.
PhysicsMathematicsCritical thinking
Start with: "The Surprising Secret of Synchronisation"
📐
3Blue1Brown
Mathematics visualised
Mathematical concepts visualised with unprecedented clarity. Changes the felt experience of mathematics from symbol manipulation to genuine understanding. The Essence of Linear Algebra and Calculus series are the finest mathematics education available at any price.
MathematicsVisualisation
Start with: "But what is a neural network?"

History & Culture

🗺️
Toldinstone
Ancient history & classics
Roman history and classical antiquity by a working classicist. Rigorous, narrative, genuinely expert. The companion to Meditations and Letters from a Stoic on the Reading List — understanding the world that produced these texts deepens them significantly.
Roman historyAntiquity
Start with: "Daily Life in Ancient Rome"
🎙️
Lex Fridman
Long-form interviews
Long-form conversations with scientists, engineers, philosophers, and public figures. 3–4 hour episodes. Best accessed as audio during commutes or exercise. The depth of engagement with technical and philosophical topics is exceptional.
Long-formSciencePhilosophy
Start with: interview with Andrej Karpathy or Roger Penrose
A curated subscription list is a curated education. Most people subscribe to what is entertaining. Some subscribe to what is important. The two categories overlap occasionally — but that overlap should be deliberately chosen, not accidentally encountered.— Von Lycaon
Every significant purchase is an allocation of finite resources toward a priority. Made deliberately, it produces value that compounds. Made impulsively, it produces clutter, debt, and the dull regret of money that could have been better directed. The following is the framework for spending intelligently — in Sarajevo, with Bosnian marks, on the actual categories that matter.— Von Lycaon

The Buying Framework

1
The Cost-Per-Use Calculation
Divide the price of any item by the number of times you will realistically use it. A 200 BAM pair of quality shoes worn 300 times costs 0.67 BAM per use. A 40 BAM pair worn 20 times costs 2 BAM per use. The cheaper item costs three times more per use. This arithmetic governs almost every quality-versus-price decision correctly.
2
Buy Once, Buy Right
For items used daily — shoes, a work bag, a coat, kitchen knives, a mattress, a desk chair — buy the best quality you can afford once and maintain it. The replacement cycle of cheap daily-use items costs more over 5 years than the premium version bought once.
3
The 48-Hour Rule
For any non-essential purchase above 50 BAM: wait 48 hours after deciding to buy. If it still seems necessary and worthwhile after 48 hours, buy it. Most impulse purchases do not survive the 48-hour test. Those that do were probably legitimate needs.
4
One In, One Out
Before buying anything for the apartment — clothing, kitchenware, technology — identify what it replaces or displaces. If nothing, question the purchase. The apartment has finite space; objects that accumulate without replacing anything eventually constitute clutter that has a cost of its own in visual noise and cleaning time.

The Three-Tier System

Tier 1Buy Premium — Daily Use Items
Items used every day that directly affect health, comfort, or productivity. The cost-per-use calculation always justifies premium quality here.
  • Shoes (work and everyday)
  • Desk chair — already covered in the Windows 11 and chair guide context
  • Mattress and pillow
  • Quality kitchen knife (one good knife)
  • Work bag or backpack
  • Winter coat
For these items: research thoroughly, buy from known brands with service infrastructure, and maintain them properly. See Furniture Care tab for maintenance.
Tier 2Mid-Range — Regular Use Items
Used several times per week. Quality matters but premium is unnecessary.
  • Casual clothing basics (Reserved, Zara)
  • Kitchen equipment beyond the primary knife
  • Electronics accessories
  • Gym or workout equipment
Reserved Sarajevo (BBI Centre, SCC) provides solid value for basics. Zara for slightly more design-forward pieces. H&M for purely functional items where appearance is secondary.
Tier 3Budget — Infrequent or Consumable
Used rarely or consumed quickly. Premium quality produces negligible benefit over budget.
  • Cleaning supplies
  • Most stationery
  • Seasonal items used 5–10 times per year
  • Commodity items (bin bags, cling film, batteries)
Konzum own-brand for consumables. Dm for hygiene and cleaning. Temu for applicable categories (see Temu Finds tab).

Sarajevo Retailer Map

BBI Centre
Clothing · Electronics · Books
Reserved, Zara, H&M, Interspar, Buybook, various international brands. The most complete retail destination in the city.
Best time: weekday mornings. Parking: BBI underground. Bookshop: Buybook on ground floor has English-language titles.
SCC (Sarajevo City Centre)
Fashion · Food court · Cinema
More fashion-oriented than BBI. Mango, Pull&Bear, Bershka, Springfield. The food court for a quick meal. Cinema for evenings.
More crowded than BBI at weekends. The underground level for electronics accessories at competitive prices.
Bauhaus (Ilidža)
Tools · Home · Garden
The complete home maintenance, garden, and construction supply retailer. Everything in the Home Maintenance tab is sourced here. Tram to Ilidža then 10-minute walk.
Weekend mornings are busiest. Weekday afternoons offer faster service. Price-match with online equivalents is sometimes possible.
dm Drogerie Markt
Pharmacy · Cosmetics · Supplements
The most important daily-use retailer in Sarajevo. Skincare, supplements (Omega-3, Vitamin D, protein powder), cleaning products, stationery. Multiple locations throughout the city.
The dm app provides loyalty points and advance notice of promotions. Significant discounts on supplements during periodic sales.
Lesnina (Ilidža)
Furniture · Home textiles · Appliances
The primary furniture and home goods retailer. The Interior Design tab's sourcing begins here. Adjacent to Bauhaus — both on the same tram journey.
End-of-season sales (February and August) offer genuine discounts on floor models. Delivery available within Sarajevo.

Budget Benchmarks — Sarajevo 2025

  • Quality everyday shoes: 120–200 BAM (Ecco, Clarks at BBI)
  • Winter coat (quality): 200–400 BAM (Reserved premium, international brands)
  • Work shirt (quality): 60–120 BAM
  • Kitchen knife (good): 80–150 BAM (Zwilling, WMF at housewares shops)
  • Desk chair (adequate): 200–400 BAM (Giga.ba, Lesnina)
  • Smartphone mid-range: 600–900 BAM
The quality of your possessions is a function of your selectivity, not your spending. The man who owns ten carefully chosen things lives better than the man who owns a hundred accumulated ones. Spend less on more things. Spend more on fewer things. The first produces clutter. The second produces a life of quality objects.— Von Lycaon
Temu is a legitimate sourcing tool for specific categories and a waste of money for others. The platform's business model — subsidised prices to acquire market share — means quality is real in some areas and absent in others. This tab is the intelligence that prevents the latter.— Von Lycaon
⚠️
Temu prices can be misleadingly low. Always check: reviews with photos (not text only), whether the size runs small (most clothing does), and whether the item serves a genuine need or merely costs too little to feel like a risk. The sum of several cheap unnecessary purchases exceeds the cost of one useful purchase.

The Vetting Protocol

1
Check the photo reviews
Text reviews are unreliable. Photo reviews showing the actual item received in real conditions are the primary quality signal. If an item has 500 reviews and no photo reviews — treat it as unverified regardless of the star rating.
2
Read the 3-star reviews
5-star reviews are optimistic. 1-star reviews are often emotional. 3-star reviews are where the honest assessment lives: "product is okay but smaller than pictured" tells you what 5-star reviews omit.
3
Check the dimensions against something you own
Product dimensions are listed in centimetres. Compare them to something you own before ordering. A "large" phone stand that is 8cm wide is useless for a large phone. The dimensions are accurate; the product name is marketing.
4
Order one before ordering many
If you need 4 of something — order 1 first. The delivery time makes this a slower process, but receiving 4 items of wrong quality costs 4 times as much as receiving 1.
5
Check the return policy for that specific item
Most Temu items are returnable within 90 days for the first return per order at no cost. Subsequent returns on the same order require you to pay shipping. Know this before ordering multiple items.

Category Verdicts

✅
Strong Buy — Consistent Quality
These categories reliably deliver value
Phone accessoriesCases, stands, holders, mounts
The manufacturing tolerances for phone cases are standardised. A case that fits an iPhone 15 Pro from Temu fits identically to one from an Apple Store. Quality varies but the category is reliable at 2–5 EUR vs 30+ EUR retail.
Cable organisers & storageVelcro ties, cable boxes, organisers
Commodity items with no meaningful quality threshold. A velcro cable tie is a velcro cable tie. Order freely.
Kitchen toolsSilicone spatulas, zesters, peelers
Simple utensils without mechanical complexity perform to standard. The silicone spatula that costs 1.50 EUR performs identically to the one costing 12 EUR. Avoid anything with a motor, heating element, or precision mechanism.
Stationery & notebooksPens, paper, organisers
Acceptable quality for everyday use. Not a replacement for the Rhodia paper or Lamy pen in the Handwriting tab — but for draft notebooks, sticky notes, and filing, the quality is sufficient at a fraction of the cost.
⚠️
Use Caution — Variable Quality
Vet carefully before ordering
ClothingT-shirts, basics, accessories
Size runs 1–2 sizes small consistently. Fabric weight is often lighter than photographed. Order one first. Check the fabric composition — anything above 60% synthetic will feel cheap and retain odour. Cotton or cotton-blend basics can be acceptable at the price point for casual wear.
Small electronicsLED lights, small gadgets
Simple LED strips and basic gadgets are acceptable. Anything with a battery, charging circuit, or motor requires more caution — substandard batteries and charging circuits are a fire risk. Stick to name-brand equivalents for anything that connects to mains power.
❌
Avoid — High Failure Rate
These categories consistently disappoint
Do not order from Temu:
  • Shoes — size inconsistency, sole separation, and comfort issues are endemic. The cost-per-use calculation fails rapidly.
  • Skincare and cosmetics — unregulated formulations, potential allergens, and no EU safety certification. The Grooming tab products are available at dm at accessible prices. Do not apply unknown compounds to your face.
  • Power tools and anything mains-powered — no CE certification equivalent, potential fire and shock risk.
  • Supplements and anything ingested — no quality control, no accurate label claims, potential contaminants.

Best Search Terms

  • Home organisation: "cable management box", "desk organiser bamboo", "drawer dividers"
  • Kitchen: "silicone spatula set", "herb stripper", "avocado slicer", "spice jar labels"
  • Tech accessories: "phone stand adjustable", "cable clips adhesive", "screen cleaning kit"
  • Fitness: "resistance bands set", "ab wheel roller", "foam roller massage", "yoga mat"
  • Stationery: "spiral notebook A5", "gel pen set", "sticky note pad"
Temu is a tool. Tools used deliberately produce useful outcomes. Tools used impulsively because the price feels like it negates the decision produce a drawer of items that were cheap in every sense of the word.— Von Lycaon
My coat, as they say, is always pristine. This is not vanity — it is respect. Respect for the people I encounter, and respect for myself. Grooming is maintenance of the primary instrument. You would not present a scratched and dusty tool to a client. The same principle applies to yourself.— Von Lycaon

Skincare — The Science and the Practice

The skin is the body's largest organ and the most visible. Most men dramatically under-invest in its maintenance, then wonder why they look tired at thirty-five. The good news is that the investment required is modest: three minutes in the morning, two minutes at night, and consistency over time. Chemistry does the rest.

How Skin Works (Briefly)

  • The stratum corneum is the outermost layer — dead skin cells in a lipid matrix. It is your first barrier. Cleansers that strip it cause dryness. Moisturisers that support its lipid content maintain it.
  • Transepidermal water loss (TEWL) is the constant process of moisture evaporating through the skin. Cold Sarajevo winters accelerate this. A good moisturiser creates an occlusive barrier that slows TEWL.
  • UV radiation is the primary cause of premature skin ageing — far more than diet, sleep, or genetics. SPF every morning is not optional. It is maintenance.
  • Ceramides are lipid molecules naturally present in healthy skin. Skincare products containing ceramides (look for this on the label) actively restore barrier function. CeraVe — if available — is named after them. Garnier Moisture+ and Vichy Normaderm both contain barrier-supporting ingredients.

The Morning Routine (3 minutes)

Step 1

Cleanse

Garnier Skin Naturals Pure Active or Vichy Normaderm Cleansing Gel — both at dm Sarajevo. Use lukewarm water, never hot. Hot water strips the skin's natural oils. Massage for thirty seconds, rinse, pat dry. Do not rub — patting preserves the skin barrier.

Step 2

Moisturise with SPF

L'Oréal Men Expert Hydra Power or Garnier Men Sensitive SPF — both at dm. Apply to a slightly damp face; this improves absorption. Use two fingertip units across the face and neck. The neck is skin too. It ages the same way. Treat it.

Step 3

Lip balm

Labello Classic or Labello Active — dm checkout, every location. The lips have no sebaceous glands and cannot moisturise themselves. In Sarajevo winters particularly, unprotected lips crack within days. One application each morning. Keep one at the desk for afternoon reapplication.

The Evening Routine (2 minutes)

Step 1

Cleanse again

Remove the day: pollution particles, excess sebum, SPF residue. The same cleanser used in the morning works here. Night cleansing is arguably more important than morning — the skin repairs itself overnight and cannot do so efficiently through a layer of accumulated grime.

Step 2

Night moisturiser

Nivea Men Intensive Moisturiser or Balea Men (dm's own brand — exceptional value, genuinely good formulation). Apply to the face and neck. The night moisturiser need not contain SPF; it should focus on hydration and barrier support. Use the extra moisture for richer application than the morning product.

Hair — Understanding Before Styling

Before discussing products, it is worth understanding your hair type. The approach that works for straight fine hair is counterproductive for curly thick hair. Most styling failures come from using the wrong product for the hair type, not from using products incorrectly.

Fine / Straight Hair

Lightweight products only. Heavy clays and waxes weigh fine hair flat. Use a light styling cream or a volume-boosting mousse. Got2b Phenomenal Matte Clay at dm works at very low amounts — a small pea-sized application is sufficient.

Thick / Coarse Hair

Needs more control and hold. Syoss Men Power Hold Styling Paste (dm/Müller) or L'Oréal Studio Line has sufficient grip. Apply to damp hair for better distribution, then shape as it dries.

Wavy / Curly Hair

Avoid products with high alcohol content — they cause frizz. A curl-defining cream applied to wet hair, left to air-dry without touching, produces the best result. Garnier Curl Sculpting Cream is available at dm.

Barbering in Sarajevo

  • Frequency: Every three to four weeks. A style allowed to grow past its form becomes not a style at all. The investment in a monthly haircut is the single highest-return grooming expenditure available.
  • Brief your barber specifically. "The same as last time" is not a brief — barbers see dozens of people per week and remember approximations, not specifics. Bring a reference photo. Specify the length on top, the fade gradient on the sides, the neckline shape.
  • The neckline: Square, round, or tapered. Square is sharp and angular; round follows the natural hairline; tapered fades gradually. Each gives a different character to the overall look. Know which you have and confirm it with each visit.

Fragrance — The Full Education

Fragrance is the most intimate and most frequently mismanaged element of a man's presentation. It is detected before appearance is registered at close range and lingers in memory long after the encounter. It is worth understanding properly.

Fragrance Families

Woody / Aromatic

Cedarwood, vetiver, sandalwood, patchouli. Warm, grounded, masculine. This family suits the character of this codex most closely. Boss Bottled (available at Plaza Parfumerija, BBI Centar) is the accessible entry point.

Fresh / Aquatic
